active_question: Which decades does Master K identify as the beginning of the modern kinbaku era?
edited_answer: He had access to original historical materials and could interview witnesses to the early modern kinbaku era, including Nureki Chimuo and Urado Hiroshi.
edited_question: Why does Master K say the historical research behind “The Beauty of Kinbaku” would be difficult to reproduce today?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement trades a bare decades lookup for the interview's substantive account of the book's primary-source and oral-history provenance.
reason: The replacement trades a bare decades lookup for the interview's substantive account of the book's primary-source and oral-history provenance.
support_evidence: “K”: I think my book came out just at the time of a worldwide explosion of interest in Kinbaku. Also, because I’ve been studying the art for over 40 years I think my book benefited from research that would be very difficult to do today, such as having access to original historical materials to say nothing of having the privilege of interviewing people like the late Nureki Chimuo sensei, the founder of the famous SM Library in Tokyo, Urado Hiroshi sensei, a close, elderly colleague of the famed martial arts expert Nawa Yumio, etc., etc. These are all people who were there at the very beginning of the modern Kinbaku era in the 1950’s and 60’s.
